summary: Round 2 resolves both prior major findings. The workflow now fully paginates account issues (and also includes claim refs and secret names), fails closed on any incomplete listing, and uses server-side first-writer-wins Git ref creation as the shared slot claim before storing a credential or creating an account issue. Collision handling re-enumerates state, non-collision failures abort, claims are retained to prevent reuse, and the manual runbook now requires the same claim protocol. Downstream steps consume the atomically claimed handle rather than the stale pre-login hint. The residual manual probe-to-upsert window is outside the protocol, but the documented manual writer is brought into the protocol and an existing secret is explicitly protected from overwrite. No blocker, major, or other actionable diff-scoped issue remains.
